steps.title: Use cylinder scaling
steps.content: A cylinder has volume $V=\pi r^2h$. If the radius is multiplied by $a$ and the height is multiplied by $b$, the volume is multiplied by $a^2b$.

steps.title: Match the volume factor
steps.content:
The new volume must be $576$ times the original volume. Since
$$576=8^2\cdot9,$$
a radius factor of $8$ and a height factor of $9$ gives the needed multiplier.

steps.title: Choose the dimensions
steps.content: The dimensions $R=8r$ and $H=9h$ make the volume scale by $8^2\cdot9=576$. The answer is C.
